revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:㯮椒神社]] {{Expand Japanese|㯮椒神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hajikami Shrine (Q11275441) | native_name = 㯮椒神社 | image = Hajikami-Jinjya shaden Takeno.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hajikami Shrine (Q11275441) | coordinates = {{coord|35.53805|134.702945}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Hajikami-no-Ōkami|lt=Hajikami-no-Ōkami|qid=Q112299557|1=Hajikami-no-Ōkami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hajikami Shrine'''|㯮椒神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tajima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11381626|ltq=Q11381626|qid=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} ** Series ordinal: 70 ** P155: {{ill|Ikazuchi Shrine|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|qid=Q21655264|1=Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135040666|ltq=Q135040666|qid=Q135040666|1=Karakuni Shrine (Q135040666)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|lt=Toyooka|ar|تويوكا (هيوغو)|azb|تویواوکا، هیوقو|az|Toyooka|bg|Тойока|ca|Toyooka|ceb|Toyooka|ce|Тоёока|de|Toyooka|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|envoyage|Toyooka|es|Toyooka (Hyōgo)|fa|تویواوکا، هیوگو|fi|Toyooka|fr|Toyooka|gl|Toyooka|he|טויואוקה|hy|Տոյոոկա (քաղաք)|idvoyage|Toyooka|it|Toyooka|ja|豊岡市|kk|Тоёока|ko|도요오카시|mg|Toyooka|mzn|تویواوکا، هیوگو|nl|Toyooka (Hyogo)|pl|Toyo’oka|pt|Toyooka|ro|Toyooka|ru|Тоёока|sv|Toyooka|tg|Тоёока|th|โทโยโอกะ|tl|Toyooka, Hyogo|tr|Toyooka|tt|Тоёока|uz|Toyooka (Hyōgo)|vi|Toyooka, Hyōgo|war|Toyooka, Hyōgo|zh_classical|豐岡市|zh_min_nan|Toyooka-chhī|zh_yue|豐岡|zh|豐岡市|zhvoyage|豐岡|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}} * {{ill|Q1053727|ltq=Q1053727|qid=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} * {{ill|Keta district|lt=Keta district|ja|気多郡 (兵庫県)|zh|氣多郡 (兵庫縣)|qid=Q21655271|1=Keta district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.53805|134.702945}} == image (P18) == * Hajikami-Jinjya shaden Takeno.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Hajikami-no-Ōkami|lt=Hajikami-no-Ōkami|qid=Q112299557|1=Hajikami-no-Ōkami}} == OpenCorporates ID (P1320) == * jp/5140005012336 == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20140103234954/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380304.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182684 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はじかみじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5140005012336 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市竹野町椒字岩内1738-2', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:㯮椒神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11275441}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hajikami Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Hajikami-Jinjya shaden Takeno.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Takeno-chō Hajikami, Aza Iwanai 1738-2 |位置 = {{Coord|35|32|16.6|N|134|42|10.3|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hajikami Ōkami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-sheet roof |別名 = Hachimangū |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Hajikami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hajikami Shrine'''The first character (㯮) is wood-radical + 蜀. (はじかみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Takeno-chō|Q7678035|qid=Q7678035|1=Takeno, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It stands on a mountain summit in the Hajikami settlement, with the Sanchō River below. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Some sources read the shrine name as "Hosoki"Side-reading notes in manuscript copies of ''Engishiki'', and also ''Hyogo Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1983), Kobe Shimbun Publishing Center.. === Enshrined deity === * '''Hajikami Ōkami''' (はじかみのおおかみ) ** Because Hajikami settlement produced {{ill|lt=Asakura sanshō|Q109598996|qid=Q109598996|1=Asakura sanshō}}, there is a theory it was worshipped as a local land deity''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}''.; another theory sees it as deification of {{ill|lt=Inuzanshō|Q15249901|qid=Q15249901|1=Zanthoxylum schinifolium}}''History of Toyooka City'' Vol.1, Toyooka City, 1981., but details are unclear. One theory says it enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyama-mori-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}"Keta District Old Tales" in ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokushi Monjo|1=UNKNOWN}}''. ''Kokushi Monjo'' is said to have been compiled in 975 by a person named Yōkō Mashika and is often treated as doubtful, though some accept old traditions within it (Keiichi Imai, ''Ame no Hiboko'', Sōgeisha, 1966). See also {{ill|lt=Koshikoden|Q11410976|qid=Q11410976|1=koshi koden}}., but its textual basis is questionedSee previous note.. === Origin === The site has long connected to the {{ill|lt=Toyooka Basin|Q11633476|qid=Q11633476|1=Toyooka Basin}} by routes crossing Banya Pass to the east or Ōta-goe to the south via {{ill|lt=Kannabe Highlands|Q11591007|qid=Q11591007|1=Kannabe Highlands}}, and via river transport on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nasa River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Inanba River|Q16277514|qid=Q16277514|1=Inanba River}} to the provincial center of {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|qid=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}}; links with the kokufu are assumed''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. It has also been pointed out that this shrine may relate to the one to (斗) of "Hajikami" medicine herb that Tajima had to present under the "miscellaneous medicines from provinces" section in the {{ill|lt=Engi medical office code|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 14 ({{ill|lt=685|Q33480|qid=Q33480|1=685}}), the Tajima provincial governor Haibara no Kimi Kagamaro enshrined his ancestor Ōyama-mori-no-Mikoto, and in the next year ({{ill|lt=Shuchō|Q777413|qid=Q777413|1=Shuchō}} 1 / {{ill|lt=686|Q30619|qid=Q30619|1=686}}) oil pressed from local sanshō fruit was presented to the imperial court; however, reliability is questioned. In {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|qid=Q24730|1=842}}), it entered the official shrine register''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'', entry for 15th day, 10th month, same year., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|qid=Q23917|1=869}}) it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for 27th day, 12th month, same year.. Under the Engi system it was listed as a myojin taisha. Notably, other Tajima myojin taisha ({{ill|lt=Yama Shrine (Toyooka)|Q21655261|qid=Q21655261|lt=Yama|1=Yama Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=To Shrine|Q21655262|qid=Q21655262|lt=To|1=Tono Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Ikazuchi Shrine (Toyooka)|Q21655264|qid=Q21655264|lt=Ikazuchi|1=Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)}}, and {{ill|lt=Watatsumi Shrine (Toyooka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumi}}) likewise entered the official register and rose to Junior Fifth Rank Upper at the same time. At an unknown date, Hachiman deity appears to have been invited from {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|qid=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} in {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}. From the medieval period onward, as "Masu (Hajikami) Betsugū," it was counted as one of the Tajima eight branch shrines of Iwashimizue.g., imperial proclamation dated Dec 3, {{ill|lt=Hōgen|Q1193898|qid=Q1193898|1=Hōgen (era)}} 3 (1158), in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū documents|1=UNKNOWN}}.. In the Tajima provincial {{ill|lt=Ōta-bumi|Q1413174|qid=Q1413174|1=Ōtabumi}} dated {{ill|lt=Kōan|Q389180|qid=Q389180|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1285|Q5546|qid=Q5546|1=1285}}), it appears as "Hachiman-gū territory, Hajikami Betsugū," showing it was the guardian shrine of an Iwashimizu estate and was henceforth mainly called "Hachimangū." It also had deep ties with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōoka-ji (Toyooka)|1=UNKNOWN}} on Mt. Ōoka's slope; records state this shrine's torii marked the northern boundary of that temple precinctDraft report on temple land and forests of Ōoka-ji, dated Aug 7, {{ill|lt=Eiryaku|Q1193895|qid=Q1193895|1=Eiryaku}} 2 (1161) ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōoka-ji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}).. In early modern times, Ōoka-ji is confirmed as its {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}munafuda of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 9 (1669), and ''Kakiagechō'' of Kyōhō 20 (1735), etc. (''Takeno Town History: Folklore/Cultural Properties/Documents'').. Records from {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1679|Q7638|qid=Q7638|1=1679}}) and {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1721|Q6913|qid=Q6913|1=1721}}) show it held 7 se of shrine land around the siteLocal place names such as "Otō-dani," "Miya-no-shita," "Dō-dani," and "Asō" are said to be remnants of shrine lands (''Takeno Town History: Folklore/Cultural Properties/Documents'').. Though mountainous, nearby gold mining by Ikuno magistrate formed a small mining settlement, and in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1834|Q7591|qid=Q7591|1=1834}}) the shrine had 140 parishioner households. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) it was ranked village shrine; in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|qid=Q7831|1=1889}}) the honden was rebuilt and a heiden newly built; in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|qid=Q5272|1=1952}}) renovations were made around the honden. === Rituals === Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taishō period|1=UNKNOWN}}, festival day was old lunar Aug 15; later it changed to Sept 15 (solar), then Oct 1, and now Oct 15. Formerly, parishioners selected ritual officials called "kagiban," and rites were likely conducted through {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|qid=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} / {{ill|lt=tōya system|Q11664931|qid=Q11664931|1=touya}}''Takeno Town History: Folklore/Cultural Properties/Documents''.. === Shrine buildings === The honden is a copper-roofed {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} rebuilt in Meiji 22. Other buildings include a tile-roofed gabled {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|qid=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}}, an {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} with kokera roofing, and a board-roofed basket hall. === Sub-shrine === * Gion Shrine - at the north foot below the main shrine; enshrines a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|qid=Q854773|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} image as honji-butsu. The Omaki-zakura stands before it. It is said there was formerly also a Hachibashira Kōjin shrine. === Cultural properties === ; Designated by Toyooka City :; Komainu :: Wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|qid=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} thought to be Muromachi period works. The {{ill|lt=A-gyō|Q137190281|qid=Q137190281|1=Agyō}} is 69 cm high; the {{ill|lt=Un-gyō|Q137190286|qid=Q137190286|1=Ungyō}} is 60 cm. Both retain color, which is rare''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 28 Hyogo Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1988.. Designated tangible cultural property of Takeno Town on Nov 15, 1977; after municipal merger became Toyooka city cultural property. :; Omaki-zakura {{Main|Omaki-zakura (Hyogo)}} :: Edo-higan tree in front of the lower shrine (Gion Shrine), estimated age 500 years (as of 1991). Height 14.5 m, root girth 7 m, trunk girth at eye height 6.05 m; crown spread 15 m east-west and 18.5 m north-south, covering about 4 ares. Designated natural monument of Takeno Town on Mar 8, 1979; after merger became Toyooka city cultural property. Designated Hyogo Prefecture {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=local monument|1=UNKNOWN}} on Mar 29, 1996. === Gallery === Hajikami-Jinjya Takeno.jpg|Torii Hajikami-Jinjya Takeno002.jpg|Komainu Omaki-Sakura takeno.jpg|Omaki-zakura === Footnotes === === Notes === {{reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{reflist|2}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'' Vol.19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Gazette'' Vol.2 (reprint of 1938 original), Rinsen Shoten, 1984 ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * ''Takeno Town History: General History'', Takeno Town, 1990 * ''Takeno Town History: Folklore/Cultural Properties/Documents'', Takeno Board of Education, 1991 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|qid=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322137.html Hajikami Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380304.html Hajikami Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE, compilation of Shinto/shrine historical materials) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hajikami Shrine (Q11275441)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hajikami Shrine (Q11275441)]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]
